title: pancratium australe
intro: Pancratium australe, also known as the Australian pancratium or red lily, is a flowering plant species native to the eastern and southern regions of Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and improving digestion. The herbal preparations of Pancratium australe typically involve boiling the plant's bulbs and roots to create a tea or decoction, which can be used to treat various ailments.
